title: GKE parity sync — re-register session playbooks, bump GUI to v1.11.0, audit storage tier, re-smoke
description: The 12-round cowork session shipped to local kind systematically. GKE got the noetl engine fix (v2.37.8 from item #11) but DID NOT get the cumulative catalog updates (rounds #2 hotels/activities, #5b anthropic model, #6 mcp/ollama playbook, #9 classifier_system_prompt single-source, #10 Path B + Tutorial 08 model fields) OR the GUI upgrade to v1.11.0 (round #3 app:form refinement). Kadyapam reproduced the gap on GKE: `cd /mcp/amadeus; tools` returns 0 tools (stale catalog), and `travel <query>` returns 'unknown command' (stale gui). Plus Kadyapam asked what shared-cache tier is actually in use on GKE — wondering if it's GCS and whether it's empty/misconfigured. This round audits the GKE deployment state, re-registers all session-touched playbooks against GKE noetl-server, bumps the GUI image to v1.11.0, smokes the 4-provider matrix through the gateway terminal to confirm parity, and documents the StorageRouter config + tier actually in use. Cap: 0 PRs (config + deploy round). Output: 1 sync issue + close-out memory.

design_brief_for_codex.context_summary: Session shipped 12 rounds to local kind. Only the noetl engine (item #11, v2.37.8) reached GKE because that round had explicit GKE deploy phases. Rounds #2-#10 deployed only to kind; they need to be re-applied to GKE for parity. The GUI image was bumped to v1.11.0 on kind in round #3; GKE was never bumped.
design_brief_for_codex.gke_context: Project: noetl-demo-19700101. Worker SA: noetl-worker-mcp@noetl-demo-19700101.iam.gserviceaccount.com (already has secretmanager.secretAccessor on anthropic-api-key + Workload Identity for Vertex AI). Cluster name: confirm via `gcloud container clusters list --project=noetl-demo-19700101`. Namespace: noetl.
design_brief_for_codex.phase_1_audit_state: Build a comparison table — local kind vs GKE — for: noetl-server image tag, noetl-worker image tag, noetl-gui image tag, catalog versions for automation/agents/travel/runtime, automation/agents/mcp/amadeus, automation/agents/mcp/vertex-ai, automation/agents/mcp/ollama. Use `kubectl --context=<gke-context>` for GKE side and `kubectl --context=kind-noetl` (or whatever the local kind context is named) for kind side. The travel runtime locally is at catalog v27 (after Path B). The mcp/ollama playbook may not exist at all on GKE if it was only registered locally in round #6.
design_brief_for_codex.phase_2_re_register_playbooks_on_gke:
Pull merged repos/ops at HEAD (the gitlink in ai-meta already points to the right commit). Re-register the four session-touched playbooks against the GKE noetl-server. The cleanest path is a kubectl port-forward + `noetl register` with NOETL_SERVER_URL pointing at the forwarded port:

```
kubectl -n noetl --context=<gke> port-forward svc/noetl-server 18082:8082 &
export NOETL_SERVER_URL=http://localhost:18082/api
noetl register repos/ops/automation/agents/travel/runtime.yaml
noetl register repos/ops/automation/agents/mcp/amadeus.yaml
noetl register repos/ops/automation/agents/mcp/vertex-ai.yaml
noetl register repos/ops/automation/agents/mcp/ollama.yaml
```

Capture the new catalog version returned for each.
design_brief_for_codex.phase_3_bump_gui_image_on_gke:
GUI v1.11.0 (ghcr.io/noetl/gui:v1.11.0) was deployed locally in round #3. Bump GKE the same way:

```
kubectl -n noetl --context=<gke> set image deployment/noetl-gui noetl-gui=ghcr.io/noetl/gui:v1.11.0
kubectl -n noetl --context=<gke> rollout status deployment/noetl-gui
```

If the helm chart manages the GUI image tag rather than direct `set image`, use the helm-managed path instead (likely via the ops/noetl helm release values — match whatever the team is using for kind redeploys but targeted at the GKE cluster).
design_brief_for_codex.phase_4_smoke_through_gateway:
After re-register + GUI bump, open the gateway URL in a browser (or `kubectl port-forward` and hit the local proxy). Reproduce Kadyapam's gateway terminal session:

  1. `cd /mcp/amadeus; tools` — expected: 5 tools listed (search_flights, search_hotels, search_locations, search_activities, get_token).
  2. `cd /travel; travel help` — expected: completes and renders the help widget.
  3. `travel flights from SFO to JFK on 2026-07-15 for 2 adults` — expected: completes, renders render_flights or render_amadeus_failure widget.
  4. `travel --provider anthropic help` — expected: effective_provider='anthropic'.
  5. `travel --provider vertex-ai help` — expected: effective_provider='vertex-ai' (Workload Identity on GKE, NOT the kind fallback).
  6. `travel --provider ollama help` — expected: either completes if ollama-bridge is deployed on GKE, OR falls back to OpenAI with provider_fallback_reason='ollama <error>'. Document which.

Capture screenshots of the inline widgets.
design_brief_for_codex.phase_5_audit_storage_tier:
Kadyapam asked what shared-cache tier is actually in use on GKE — wondering if GCS is involved and whether it's empty/misconfigured. Inspect READ-ONLY:

```
# Worker env vars relevant to TempStore / StorageRouter
kubectl -n noetl --context=<gke> exec deploy/noetl-worker -- env | grep -E '(NOETL_TEMPSTORE|STORAGE|GCS|NATS|TIER)'

# Server env vars
kubectl -n noetl --context=<gke> exec deploy/noetl-server -- env | grep -E '(NOETL_TEMPSTORE|STORAGE|GCS|NATS|TIER)'

# Inspect the actual tier picked at runtime by introspecting the router
kubectl -n noetl --context=<gke> exec deploy/noetl-worker -- python -c "
from noetl.core.storage.router import default_router
from noetl.core.storage.models import StoreTier
print('router class:', default_router.__class__.__name__)
print('available tiers:', [t.value for t in StoreTier])
for attr in ('tiers', 'tier_config', 'backends', 'default_tier'):
    if hasattr(default_router, attr):
        print(f'{attr}:', getattr(default_router, attr))
"
```

Document: (a) which tier large payloads spill to on GKE (postgres? NATS? GCS?), (b) whether that tier has actual entries (`SELECT COUNT(*) FROM noetl.temp_ref WHERE execution_id IN (recent activities executions)` if it's postgres-backed), (c) whether GCS is even in the configured tier set.

If GCS is NOT in the tier set: that answers Kadyapam's question — there's no GCS cache; large payloads go to whatever is configured (likely postgres or NATS). If GCS IS in the set but the bucket is empty: deeper config issue (bucket name? IAM? misconfigured router?). If GCS isn't set up but you think it SHOULD be: document the gap as a deferred follow-up.

Note: item #11's hydration fix walks the EVENTS table directly (always postgres on GKE), bypassing the tier layer entirely for terminal-result hydration. The tier question is orthogonal to item #11's fix — it's about result CACHING for re-reads, not about hydration. But the user wants clarity on what's actually deployed.
design_brief_for_codex.phase_6_close_out:
Write the sync issue and result file. Sync issue at `sync/issues/2026-05-11-gke-deployment-drift-and-storage-tier-audit.md` covering:
  - The parity gap between local kind and GKE that surfaced this round
  - The fixes applied (re-register + GUI bump)
  - The StorageRouter findings + answer to 'is GCS the cache?'
  - Recommendation: any future round that ships to kind should have an explicit GKE-deploy phase OR a separate GKE-parity round chained on.

Also flag the ollama-bridge missing on GKE if phase 4's ollama smoke fell back to OpenAI — that's the same item from item #11's GKE smoke.

1_audit_state.objective: Build kind-vs-GKE comparison table for image tags + catalog versions.
spec: Identify the GKE kubectl context name (`kubectl config get-contexts | grep noetl-demo-19700101`).
Pull noetl-server, noetl-worker, noetl-gui image tags from BOTH clusters via kubectl get deployment -o jsonpath.
Pull catalog versions for the four session-touched playbooks via the noetl API on each cluster (port-forward as needed). API: `GET /api/catalog?path=<path>`.
Document the table in the result file.
1_audit_state.verify: Comparison table present in result file.

2_re_register_playbooks_on_gke.objective: Bring the GKE catalog up to local-kind parity for the four session-touched playbooks.
spec: Pull merged repos/ops at HEAD (ai-meta gitlink should already point at the right commit).
Port-forward GKE noetl-server: `kubectl -n noetl port-forward svc/noetl-server 18082:8082`.
Export NOETL_SERVER_URL=http://localhost:18082/api.
Run `noetl register` for: automation/agents/travel/runtime, automation/agents/mcp/amadeus, automation/agents/mcp/vertex-ai, automation/agents/mcp/ollama.
Capture new catalog versions in the result file.
Close the port-forward when done.
2_re_register_playbooks_on_gke.verify: Four playbooks registered on GKE at the same versions (or newer) as local kind.

3_bump_gui_image_on_gke.objective: Update GKE noetl-gui deployment to v1.11.0.
spec: Match the deploy mechanism used for kind. If helm-managed, use the helm upgrade path with the new image tag. If direct kubectl set image, use that.
`kubectl -n noetl rollout status deployment/noetl-gui` — confirm new pod healthy.
Capture old and new image tags in the result file.

4_smoke_through_gateway.objective: Reproduce Kadyapam's reported failures on the bumped GKE and confirm they're fixed.
spec: Open the GKE gateway URL in a browser (or port-forward and hit it).
Reproduce the original failing sequence: `cd /mcp/amadeus; tools` — expected: 5 tools listed (was 0 before).
`travel help` — expected: completes with help widget (was 'unknown command').
`travel flights from SFO to JFK on 2026-07-15 for 2 adults` — expected: completes with widget.
Three provider regression: `travel --provider anthropic help`, `travel --provider vertex-ai help`, `travel --provider ollama help`. Capture effective_provider per smoke; note any fallbacks.

Item #11 regression: `travel --provider openai activities near Times Square` — expected: render_activities widget (not render_amadeus_failure) with real data. This is the proof that v2.37.8's hydration fix is live on GKE AND the catalog has the activities branch wired.
Capture screenshots.
4_smoke_through_gateway.verify: All five GUI-flow smokes complete with correct widgets. Item #11 hydration regression clean.

5_audit_storage_tier.objective: Document what TempStore tier is actually in use on GKE; answer 'is GCS the cache'.
spec: Read-only env inspection on both noetl-server and noetl-worker pods (grep for NOETL_TEMPSTORE / STORAGE / GCS / NATS / TIER).
Run the introspection Python snippet to print router class, available tiers, and configured backends.
If postgres-backed: `SELECT count(*), MIN(created_at), MAX(created_at) FROM noetl.temp_ref WHERE execution_id IN (recent activities exec ids)` to confirm spillover happens.
Answer in result file: (a) which tier large payloads spill to on GKE, (b) is the spillover tier being populated (has entries), (c) is GCS in the tier set at all.
If GCS is expected but not configured: document as deferred follow-up sync issue.
5_audit_storage_tier.verify: Tier in use is named and documented. GCS-as-cache question answered.

6_write_sync_issue_and_close_out.objective: Sync issue + result file + memory entry.
spec: Write `sync/issues/2026-05-11-gke-deployment-drift-and-storage-tier-audit.md` covering the parity gap surfacing, the fixes applied, and the StorageRouter findings.
Result file at `bridge/outbox/20260511-110000-gke-parity-sync.result.json`.
Memory entry at `memory/inbox/2026/05/20260511-110000-gke-parity-sync-...md`.
If verdict GREEN, append paragraph to bridge/outbox/codex-spike-green-validation.md.
Flag ollama-bridge missing on GKE if phase 4 confirmed it.

7_bump_ai_meta_pointers_if_needed.objective: If any submodule pointer needs bumping (unlikely for this round since it's config-only).
spec: Likely no pointer bumps needed — round is config + deploy + audit, not code changes.
If unexpected (a playbook needed an in-flight fix during re-register), document and bump that pointer only.
Don't push ai-meta.
7_bump_ai_meta_pointers_if_needed.verify: ai-meta state matches expectation (no bumps usually).

expected_state.description: GKE noetl-gui is on v1.11.0. All four session-touched playbooks are registered at GKE catalog versions matching local kind. Gateway terminal accepts `travel <query>` and shows 5 tools at /mcp/amadeus. StorageRouter config + actual tier in use is documented.
expected_state.verify_pattern: Result file's phase 4 shows all five smokes GREEN. Phase 5 names the actual tier in use and answers the GCS question explicitly.

denied_tools: Modifying repos/noetl, repos/ops, repos/gui, repos/docs, repos/e2e — config + deploy + audit round, no code changes
Deploying ollama-bridge to GKE in this round — that's its own infra round if Kadyapam wants it
Modifying GCS bucket configuration or IAM bindings — read-only inspection only
preferred_tools: kubectl --context=<gke> for read inspection and `set image` / `rollout status` for the GUI bump
noetl register / noetl catalog list via the port-forwarded URL
Browser for gateway terminal smokes (or curl + jq for headless verification)
psql via kubectl exec on the postgres pod for temp_ref table inspection if postgres-backed
result_file_instructions: Write bridge/outbox/20260511-110000-gke-parity-sync.result.json. Required fields: {id, from: 'codex', to: 'claude-cowork', completed_at, overall_status, phases: {1_audit_state: {kind_vs_gke_table: {...}}, 2_re_register_playbooks_on_gke: {travel_version, amadeus_version, vertex_ai_version, ollama_version}, 3_bump_gui_image_on_gke: {old_image, new_image, pod_healthy: bool}, 4_smoke_through_gateway: {amadeus_tools_count, travel_help_status, flights_status, anthropic_status, vertex_ai_status, ollama_status, activities_hydration_proof: bool, screenshots: []}, 5_audit_storage_tier: {tier_in_use, gcs_configured: bool, gcs_in_tier_set: bool, spillover_populated: bool, env_vars_relevant: [], notes}, 6_write_sync_issue_and_close_out: {sync_issue_path, memory_entry_path, validation_log_appended: bool}, 7_bump_ai_meta_pointers_if_needed: {pointers_bumped: []}}, regressions_found, iterations, verdict, notes, deferred_followups: ['Deploy ollama-bridge to GKE if real Ollama inference is wanted', 'Add explicit GKE-deploy phase to any future round that ships to kind', 'GCS-backed result cache if not already configured (decide first if needed)']}. If verdict GREEN, append paragraph to bridge/outbox/codex-spike-green-validation.md.
